verdict_reasoning: EREG encodes epiregulin, an EGF-family ligand that is synthesized as a single-pass type-I transmembrane precursor (~46 kDa). This pro-epiregulin is expressed on the cell surface and mediates juxtacrine signaling before being shed by sheddases (e.g., ADAM10/ADAM17) to release the mature soluble ~18 kDa ligand that binds EGFR/ERBB4. The dominant biological actor is the shed soluble form; the TM precursor stage is real but transient. This fits the 'transient TM precursor of a shed-ligand-dominant gene' pattern, qualifying as contextual/dual_localization.
